body.label: source.root.thm
body.claim: Let $X = \mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}$ and let $\mathcal{B} = \{ B_n \mid n \in X \}$, where $B_n = \{ k \cdot n \mid k \in \mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1} \}$. In the topology on $X$ generated by $\mathcal{B}$, for every fixed $m \in X$, the closure of $\{m\}$ is $\overline{\{m\}}=\{d \in X \mid d \text{ divides } m\}$.

proof_steps.claim: Let $X = \mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1}$ and let $\mathcal{B} = \{ B_n \mid n \in X \}$, where $B_n = \{ k \cdot n \mid k \in \mathbb{Z}_{\ge 1} \}$. In the topology on $X$ generated by $\mathcal{B}$, for every fixed $m \in X$, the closure of $\{m\}$ is $\overline{\{m\}}=\{d \in X \mid d \text{ divides } m\}$.
